title: What is an Array? 🗄️
description: Store multiple pieces of data in a single box.

slides.title: Too Many Variables 🤯
The Filing Cabinet 🗄️
slides.content:
## Arrays

An **Array** is like a filing cabinet. One big variable that can hold many pieces of data inside it, neatly organized in order.

quizData.question: What symbol tells Java you are creating an Array instead of a normal variable?
quizData.explanation: You add square brackets after the data type (`int[]`) to signal it's an array of integers.

slides.title: Fixed Length 📏
slides.content:
## The Golden Rule of Arrays

In Java, standard Arrays have a **Fixed Length**. 

If you create an array that holds 5 items, it will FOREVER hold exactly 5 items. You cannot add a 6th item later, and you cannot shrink it to hold only 4.

quizData.question: In Java, standard Arrays can magically stretch to fit more items if you run out of room.
quizData.explanation: Standard Arrays in Java are strictly fixed in size. Shrinking or stretching them requires creating a brand new array!
